name: HRC_LOADER_BATCH_HEADER
description: The header table is the top level table for a batch data load.

columns.Name: LOADER_BATCH_ID
columns.Comments: LOADER_BATCH_ID

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: LOADER_AM_NAME
columns.Comments: LOADER_AM_NAME

columns.Name: LOADER_AM_CONFIG_NAME
columns.Comments: LOADER_AM_CONFIG_NAME

columns.Name: BATCH_STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the batch load job.

columns.Name: BATCH_STATUS_TEXT
columns.Comments: Status description for the batch load job.

columns.Name: VALIDATE_ONLY
columns.Comments: If true, then only validate data and no data base commit.

columns.Name: NUM_THREADS
columns.Comments: Number of threads to process job.

columns.Name: CURRENT_CHUNK_NUM
columns.Comments: Current chunk being processed if running in parallel.

columns.Name: TOTAL_NUM_CHUNKS
columns.Comments: Total number of discrete chunks of data in the batch lines table.

columns.Name: USER_ID
columns.Comments: User id of the user to start the batch load job.

columns.Name: REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: Enterprise Service Scheduler: indicates the request ID of the job that created or last updated the row.

columns.Name: JOB_DEFINITION_NAME
columns.Comments: Enterprise Service Scheduler: indicates the name of the job that created or last updated the row.

columns.Name: JOB_DEFINITION_PACKAGE
columns.Comments: Enterprise Service Scheduler: indicates the package name of the job that created or last updated the row.

columns.Name: NOTIFICATION_CLASS
columns.Comments: Full path name for the class used to notify pre and post processing for this batch.

columns.Name: UPDATE_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: When defining the column, the DB column comments should be "Enterprise Service Scheduler: indicates the request ID of the job that last updated the row."

columns.Name: XFER_JOB_ID
columns.Comments: Holds the file transfer identifier if the header was created from due to file processing.

columns.Name: BATCH_NAME
columns.Comments: Batch Name

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
